exercises.whyThisExercise: This exercise targets the back muscles effectively while being safe for those with back pain. It helps in building a strong and wide back.
exercises.coachTip: Focus on a controlled motion and squeeze your shoulder blades together.

exercises.whyThisExercise: Seated cable rows are great for targeting the mid-back and are safe for back pain. They enhance back thickness and improve posture.
exercises.coachTip: Keep your back straight and pull with your elbows.

exercises.whyThisExercise: Dumbbell rows help in isolating each side of the back for balanced muscle development. They are effective for building muscle mass.
exercises.coachTip: Ensure a full range of motion and keep your core engaged.

exercises.whyThisExercise: Face pulls target the rear delts and upper back, crucial for shoulder health and posture. They are safe and effective for back pain.
exercises.coachTip: Keep your elbows high and focus on squeezing your shoulder blades.

exercises.whyThisExercise: Dead bugs are excellent for core stability without straining the back. They help in building a strong core foundation.
exercises.coachTip: Maintain a neutral spine and control your breathing.

sessionNote: Progressive overload is key — aim to beat last session by at least 1 rep or 2.5kg. All muscle groups are fully recovered, so focus on form and technique. Remember to adjust weights if needed.
dayCoachTip: Ensure proper form to protect your back and maximize muscle engagement.

cardioSuggestion.reason: A brisk walk aids recovery and enhances cardiovascular health.
